summary: Computes the POSIX-style relative path needed to navigate from one supplied path to another.
signature_intent: Accepts two path strings and returns a string representing the relative POSIX path between them.
behavior: Validates that both inputs are paths before processing.
Returns an empty string when both paths identify the same location.
Resolves both inputs to absolute POSIX paths before comparison.
Returns the destination suffix when the origin is an ancestor of the destination.
Returns one or more parent-directory segments when navigation must move upward.
Combines upward navigation with the remaining destination path when the paths diverge.
Uses forward slashes as path separators.
edge_cases: Equivalent paths after resolution return an empty string.
Identical input strings return an empty string before resolution.
Paths with shared prefixes but different path segments are treated as divergent paths.
Root-relative navigation may return only the remaining destination path or parent-directory traversal as appropriate.
forbidden_details: Do not mention internal variable names.
Do not mention repository names or source paths.
Do not describe low-level character scanning or index bookkeeping.
